title: Embargoed race-blocked OOF for every encoder, stacker, and calibrator
summary: Recent leakage work on grouped/temporal data shows near-duplicate neighbors can make CV materially optimistic.
rationale: Recent leakage work on grouped/temporal data shows near-duplicate neighbors can make CV materially optimistic. This dataset has dense within-race driver sequences, so any target encoding, stacking layer, or calibrator built without strict race blocking can silently learn future-adjacent patterns.
implementation_hint: Use folds blocked by full Race_Year, and when producing OOF features add a lap-neighborhood embargo so adjacent laps from the same driver/race never contribute to validation-side encoders or meta-models. Refit historical priors, stackers, and calibrators only on fold-pure OOF predictions.
expected_effect: May not raise nominal local CV immediately, but it should reduce false lifts and improve transfer from local validation to public leaderboard.
risk: Scores may drop at first because the estimate gets harsher; with only 26 races, fold count and sample efficiency become tighter.
